fields.summary: Expose Node Attributes info via RM nodes REST API

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: Query node-attributes info via nodes REST API via RM.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body:
Tested one a single node cluster. Built from source, setup the

cluster, set following configuration:
{noformat}
<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider</name>
  <value>config</value>
 </property>

<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider.fetch-interval-ms</name>
  <value>1000</value>
 </property>

<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider.configured-node-attributes</name>
  <value>host,STRING,hdpdevm1:os,STRING,RHEL_7_2</value>
 </property>

{noformat}
access via http://<RM:PORT>/ws/v1/cluster/nodes, result:   !node_attributes_via_config.png!

Thanks [~cheersyang]

Some doubts
 # We have not used *default* in RMNode yet. This is one of first such use. I still prefer old way here as RMNode is one of most common used common interface and we can keep it in line with branch-2 as possible.
 # I think we will be getting a mix of DIST and CENTRALIZED attributes together  from NodeInfo now. I think its perfectly fine for now. we will later add new apis to get attributes per type, per node etc.
 # Some test cases ?

Other than #1/#3, I am good with patch.

Hi [~sunilg]

I have removed the "default" in RMNode interface as you suggested in v3 patch. Regarding #2, you are correct, query by filter is probably task for future, right now it's helpful to display all attributes that is aware by RM. Regarding #3, since the change is pretty straightforward and I have tested on a cluster, I haven't included an UT in this patch. Is that OK?

Thanks.

bq.Regarding #3, since the change is pretty straightforward and I have tested on a cluster, I haven't included an UT in this patch. Is that OK?

Thanks for verifying in real cluster. If you are not planning to add UT here, cud u pls raise another ticket so that we wont miss it in future.

Hi [~sunilg]

Sure, I have created YARN-8101 to track the UT. I will post a patch when I get some free time.

Thank you.
Looks good. Committing shortly.

Tested one a single node cluster. Built from source, setup the

cluster, set following configuration:
{noformat}
<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider</name>
  <value>config</value>
 </property>

<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider.fetch-interval-ms</name>
  <value>1000</value>
 </property>

<property>
  <name>yarn.nodemanager.node-attributes.provider.configured-node-attributes</name>
  <value>host,STRING,hdpdevm1:os,STRING,RHEL_7_2</value>
 </property>

{noformat}
access via http://<RM:PORT>/ws/v1/cluster/nodes, result:   !node_attributes_via_config.png!

Thanks [~cheersyang]

Some doubts
 # We have not used *default* in RMNode yet. This is one of first such use. I still prefer old way here as RMNode is one of most common used common interface and we can keep it in line with branch-2 as possible.
 # I think we will be getting a mix of DIST and CENTRALIZED attributes together  from NodeInfo now. I think its perfectly fine for now. we will later add new apis to get attributes per type, per node etc.
 # Some test cases ?

Other than #1/#3, I am good with patch.

Hi [~sunilg]

I have removed the "default" in RMNode interface as you suggested in v3 patch. Regarding #2, you are correct, query by filter is probably task for future, right now it's helpful to display all attributes that is aware by RM. Regarding #3, since the change is pretty straightforward and I have tested on a cluster, I haven't included an UT in this patch. Is that OK?

Thanks.
